citations.narrative:
During inspection, one brown and white rabbit was found in the corner of an outdoor enclosure, shivering, and away from
the other nine rabbits. The shivering was even more evident when the inspector put their hand on the rabbit’s back and
felt it tremoring. The ambient temperature measured 35.8 degrees Fahrenheit (via the inspector’s kestrel) at about 8 AM
during this inspection. Last night, a freeze warning was in effect and reached a low of 29 degrees with a high temperature
of 46 degrees today. Another freeze warning was issued for the evening of the inspection day, with a low temperature of
30 degrees expected, per WeatherBug. There was inadequate bedding and/or shelter provided within this enclosure for
the climatic conditions. The entire floor of the enclosure was wet dirt, and most of the rabbits all had wet feet. Two plastic
hutches, both circular shelters (appeared as upside-down buckets/planters approximately 2 feet in diameter), had wet dirt
floors and one had two large holes on the top and on one side where it had cracked. A third hutch, a plastic outdoor
rectangular doghouse (approximately 30 inches long by 18 inches wide) was caked with mud and excrement on its floor.
Pop-up fabric tunnels, designed for indoor use, were also within the enclosure but only one fabric tunnel was intact and
measured approximately 10 inches in diameter and 48 inches long with a secondary connecting "T" tunnel of
approximately 12 inches. The intact fabric tunnel was uniformly wet along the floor area touching the ground and the three
other fabric tunnels within the enclosure were in such disrepair, missing most of the fabric and tattered, they were non-
functional and lying in pieces on the ground. The hutches provided were not adequate to keep this rabbit from being cold.
One rabbit was affected at the time of the inspection, and the other nine rabbits were also at risk of being cold since the
temperature was to drop below freezing the evening of the inspection day, per WeatherBug. Not taking appropriate
measures to alleviate the impact of the freezing climatic conditions may be detrimental for the rabbits’ overall health and
well-being which could lead to illness as well as animal discomfort. Appropriate measures, such as more bedding, a heat
source, and/or additional appropriate shelter, must be taken to alleviate the impact of the freezing temperatures on the
rabbits so they may comfortably maintain their body temperature.
Correct by end of day March 17, 2026.
A complete routine inspection was conducted starting on March 17, 2026. This inspection report is limited to the Direct
noncompliance identified during that inspection. An additional inspection report will be delivered with any other
noncompliant items identified during the inspection.
This inspection and exit interview for this direct noncompliance was conducted with the licensee.
n

narrative:
2.131(e)  Direct
   
Handling of animals. 
During inspection, one brown and white rabbit was found in the corner of an outdoor enclosure, shivering, and away from 
the other nine rabbits. The shivering was even more evident when the inspector put their hand on the rabbit’s back and 
felt it tremoring. The ambient temperature measured 35.8 degrees Fahrenheit (via the inspector’s kestrel) at about 8 AM 
during this inspection. Last night, a freeze warning was in effect and reached a low of 29 degrees with a high temperature 
of 46 degrees today. Another freeze warning was issued for the evening of the inspection day, with a low temperature of 
30 degrees expected, per WeatherBug. There was inadequate bedding and/or shelter provided within this enclosure for 
the climatic conditions. The entire floor of the enclosure was wet dirt, and most of the rabbits all had wet feet. Two plastic 
hutches, both circular shelters (appeared as upside-down buckets/planters approximately 2 feet in diameter), had wet dirt 
floors and one had two large holes on the top and on one side where it had cracked. A third hutch, a plastic outdoor 
rectangular doghouse (approximately 30 inches long by 18 inches wide) was caked with mud and excrement on its floor. 
Pop-up fabric tunnels, designed for indoor use, were also within the enclosure but only one fabric tunnel was intact and 
measured approximately 10 inches in diameter and 48 inches long with a secondary connecting "T" tunnel of 
approximately 12 inches. The intact fabric tunnel was uniformly wet along the floor area touching the ground and the three 
other fabric tunnels within the enclosure were in such disrepair, missing most of the fabric and tattered, they were non-
functional and lying in pieces on the ground. The hutches provided were not adequate to keep this rabbit from being cold. 
One rabbit was affected at the time of the inspection, and the other nine rabbits were also at risk of being cold since the 
temperature was to drop below freezing the evening of the inspection day, per WeatherBug. Not taking appropriate 
measures to alleviate the impact of the freezing climatic conditions may be detrimental for the rabbits’ overall health and 
well-being which could lead to illness as well as animal discomfort. Appropriate measures, such as more bedding, a heat 
source, and/or additional appropriate shelter, must be taken to alleviate the impact of the freezing temperatures on the 
rabbits so they may comfortably maintain their body temperature. 
Correct by end of day March 17, 2026. 
 
A complete routine inspection was conducted starting on March 17, 2026. This inspection report is limited to the Direct 
noncompliance identified during that inspection. An additional inspection report will be delivered with any other 
noncompliant items identified during the inspection.  
 
This inspection and exit interview for this direct noncompliance was conducted with the licensee.
